title: Christopher Manning: Linguistics and the Development of NLP
text:
Section Title:  > Christopher Manning: Linguistics and the Development of NLP
Content:
[Chris’s webpage](https://nlp.stanford.edu/~manning/)
Papers (1990s-2000s)
[Distributional Phrase Structure Induction](https://nlp.stanford.edu/~manning/papers/klein_and_manning-distributional_phrase_structure_induction-CoNLL_2001.pdf)
[Fast exact inference with a factored model for Natural Language Parsing](https://nlp.stanford.edu/manning/papers/lex-parser.pdf)
[Accurate Unlexicalized Parsing](https://nlp.stanford.edu/manning/papers/unlexicalized-parsing.pdf)
[Corpus-based induction of syntactic structure](https://nlp.stanford.edu/manning/papers/factored-induction-camera.pdf)
[Foundations of Statistical Natural Language Processing](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=1zmDOdwAAAAJ&citation_for_view=1zmDOdwAAAAJ:52Op3ZsrznwC)
Papers (2010s):
[Recursive Deep Models for Semantic Compositionality Over a Sentiment

Section Title: ... > Share this post
Content:
**Have suggestions for future podcast guests (or other feedback)? Let us know [here](https://forms.gle/jAtWTh4qheASVcDp7) !**
In episode 41 of The Gradient Podcast, [Daniel Bashir](https://twitter.com/spaniel_bashir) speaks to [Christopher Manning](https://twitter.com/chrmanning?lang=en) .
Chris is the Director of the Stanford AI Lab and an Associate Director of the Stanford Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute. He is an ACM Fellow, an AAAI Fellow, and past President of ACL. His work currently focuses on applying deep learning to natural language processing; it has included tree recursive neural networks, GloVe, neural machine translation, and computational linguistic approaches to parsing, among other topics.
